landing.headline: Turn your study materials into an active exam-prep workspace
landing.body: Upload your PDFs and slides. Get a knowledge map with intentional gaps, a tutor that stays grounded in your sources, and quizzes built from your actual material.

auth.workspaceSubtitle: Sign in to your TinkeTutor workspace

errors.invalidCredential: The email or password is incorrect.
errors.emailInUse: An account already exists for this email.
errors.popupClosed: The Google sign-in window was closed before completion.
errors.tooManyRequests: Too many attempts were made. Try again in a moment.
errors.statusLoad: Authentication status could not be loaded.

dashboard.notebookTitlePlaceholder: e.g. Danish Contract Law — Exam Prep
dashboard.firstNotebookBody: Upload your study materials and turn them into an interactive learning workspace with knowledge maps, quizzes, and Socratic tutoring.

workspace.uploadPrompt: Upload a PDF, PPTX, or DOCX source to begin processing.

sourcesPage.title: Sources
sourcesPage.libraryBody: Upload sources, review readiness, and inspect supporting excerpts for tutor citations.
sourcesPage.dropzoneMeta: PDF, PPTX, DOCX · up to 50 MB each · {count}/5 sources
sourcesPage.emptyGuidance: Upload at least one processed source before using the knowledge map, tutor flow, or quiz flow.

knowledgeMapPage.needsSourcesBody: The knowledge map is generated from notebook-ready sources. Upload and process at least one source before creating the map.
knowledgeMapPage.generateBody: The first pass is deterministic, notebook-scoped, and evidence-backed. Concepts and relationships with weak support are marked as tentative rather than stated confidently.
knowledgeMapPage.generateError: Failed to generate the knowledge map
knowledgeMapPage.failedTitle: The knowledge map could not be grounded
knowledgeMapPage.failedFallback: The notebook does not yet contain enough grounded evidence to assemble a knowledge map.

knowledgeMapInspector.skeletonBody: This node is intentionally incomplete. Use the inspector to turn a guided placeholder into a student-authored concept.
knowledgeMapInspector.completedBody: This node has already been completed or grounded. You can still refine the wording without changing the knowledge map interaction model.
knowledgeMapInspector.conceptReviewBody: This concept should be reviewed against the cited evidence.
knowledgeMapInspector.conceptGroundedBody: This concept is directly grounded in notebook evidence.
knowledgeMapInspector.edgeReviewBody: This relationship is tentative and should be refined if needed.
knowledgeMapInspector.edgeGroundedBody: This relationship is directly grounded in notebook evidence.
knowledgeMapInspector.summaryPlaceholder: Write the grounded concept in your own words.
knowledgeMapInspector.notePlaceholder: Add a concise notebook-scoped note or reminder.

knowledgeMapNode.completePrompt: Complete this concept in the inspector using notebook evidence.
knowledgeMapNode.refinePrompt: Open the inspector to refine this concept.

tutorPage.title: Socratic Tutor
tutorPage.body: Use the study support panel on the right to ask a notebook-scoped question. The tutor starts with a prompt, points you back to evidence, and only gives a direct answer after explicit escalation.
tutorPage.readySourcesOne: 1 ready source is available for guided tutoring.
tutorPage.readySourcesMany: {count} ready sources are available for guided tutoring.
tutorPage.readySourcesNone: No ready sources are available yet.
tutorPage.readinessBody: The tutor will not overstate confidence when the notebook lacks traceable support. Evidence and citations stay visible in the right rail for every grounded tutor turn.
tutorPage.step1: 1. Clarify the goal or anchor the question in the notebook.
tutorPage.step2: 2. Offer a first hint, then a stronger hint only when asked.
tutorPage.step3: 3. Point back to the evidence and ask for your own explanation.
tutorPage.step4: 4. Give a direct answer only after explicit escalation and only when the notebook supports it.

tutorPanel.selectionFallbackNode: Ask the tutor to unpack this concept without leaving notebook evidence.
tutorPanel.selectionFallbackEdge: Ask the tutor to unpack this relationship without leaving notebook evidence.
tutorPanel.studyQuestionPlaceholder: Ask a notebook-scoped study question. The tutor will guide before answering.
tutorPanel.needReadySource: Upload at least one ready source before starting a tutor session.
tutorPanel.howItWorksBody: The tutor is notebook-scoped. It starts with a prompt or hint, points you back to cited passages, and only gives a direct answer after explicit escalation.
tutorPanel.selectionFocusNode: Help me understand {label} using only notebook evidence.
tutorPanel.selectionFocusEdge: Help me understand the relationship {sourceLabel} {label} {targetLabel} using only notebook evidence.
tutorPanel.focusError: Failed to focus tutor on selection

evidencePanel.title: Evidence
evidencePanel.notebookQueryPlaceholder: Ask a precise question that should be answered only from this notebook.
evidencePanel.emptyState: Retrieval results will appear here as evidence cards with source traceability. This panel does not answer on its own; it shows what the notebook can and cannot support.

quizPage.title: Quiz
quizPage.body: Generate notebook-scoped questions with exact citations and review each answer against the supporting source material.
quizPage.needSource: Upload and process at least one source before generating a quiz.
quizPage.generatingBody: Evidence retrieval is notebook-scoped and citation-preserving. The page will update automatically when the batch is ready.
quizPage.uploadFirstBody: Quiz generation only starts once at least one source is processed and ready for notebook-scoped retrieval.
quizPage.emptyFocusedBatchBody: Generate a focused batch to turn this gap into a short targeted quiz.
quizPage.emptyBatchBody: Generate a quiz to test your current understanding against the notebook evidence.

gapsPage.title: Gap Hunter
gapsPage.body: Topic-level learning gaps are assembled from unfilled skeleton nodes, incorrect quiz attempts, and untouched notebook evidence.
gapsPage.needSource: Process at least one notebook source before running gap analysis.
gapsPage.uploadFirstBody: Gap analysis only runs after at least one source is processed and ready for grounded retrieval.
gapsPage.analyzingBody: Gap Hunter is aggregating unfilled knowledge map skeletons, quiz misses, and untouched source coverage into topic-level remediation targets.
gapsPage.noReportBody: Run the analysis to identify topic-level learning gaps tied directly to notebook evidence and current study activity.
gapsPage.noGapsBody: Current notebook signals do not show unresolved topic gaps. Generate again after more quiz activity or after editing the knowledge map.
